experiments.reasoning: The passage describes a deliberate observation of a mushroom with specific characteristics (size, shape, juice properties, and reactions to touch and drying). It includes systematic descriptions of the mushroom’s appearance, behavior when cut, and the properties of its juice, which are indicative of an experimental observation.
experiments.description: Mr. Lister observed a large, round mushroom with a thick, fleshy footstalk and open gills underneath. When cut, the mushroom released a white, milk-like juice that tasted extremely hot (like pepper), was not clammy to the touch, and did not discolor the knife blade. The juice was also noted to congeal and dry into a firm cake over days while retaining its sharp, bitter taste and white color. Additionally, the mushrooms were observed to be infested with fly maggots when full of juice.
experiments.quote: In these Woods I then found an infinite number of Mushrooms, some wither'd, and others new-sprung and flourishing. They were of a large size, something bigger than the Ordinary red-gilled eatable Mushroom or Champignon, and very much of their shape... If you cut any part of this Mushroom, it will bleed exceeding freely a Milk-white Juice, concerning which note; 1. That this Milky Juice tastes much hotter upon the tongue than Pepper. 2. That it is not clammy to the touch. 3. That the Air does not much discolour it, or the blade of a knife; as is usual with most Vegetable Juices. 4. That it became in the glass-viol, I drew it into, suddenly concret and stiff, and did in some daies dry into a firm Cake. 5. That it then also, when well dried, retain'd its fierce biting tast and white colour.
experiments.outcome: The juice's properties were noted to congeal and dry into a firm cake while retaining its sharp, bitter taste and white appearance. The presence of fly maggots was also observed when the mushrooms were full of juice.
experiments.location: Marton woods, under Pinno-moor in Craven
